title: Immunization, DPT (% of children ages 12-23 months)
definition: Child immunization, DPT, measures the percentage of children ages 12-23 months who received DPT vaccinations before 12 months or at any time before the survey. A child is considered adequately immunized against diphtheria, pertussis (or whooping cough), and tetanus (DPT) after receiving three doses of vaccine.

editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Turkiye, Portugal, Korea, Dem. People's Rep., while the lightest include Sudan, Central African Republic, Papua New Guinea; that contrast is the fastest way to read the dpt immunization pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: DPT immunization gives another view of routine health coverage, though it can resemble measles immunization.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Hepatitis B immunization can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Measles immunization can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Open defecation lives in the same category, so it can feel plausible until the high and low countries are checked.

editorial.difficultyReason: Standard difficulty: the map has readable regional structure, but the best answer still depends on checking the unit and country anchors.
regionalSignals: Health maps often separate income, public-health capacity, and conflict exposure.

reviewNotes: Useful health-system practice map, but very close to measles immunization.
